articles.title: A FRAMEWORK FOR CHOOSING THE RIGHT PLG BILLING MODEL
articles.summary: For product-led growth to succeed, it needs to be easy for customers to use your product — without putting your revenue at risk. Metronome's new blog [4] offers a framework for choosing PLG billing models based on company safeguards vs customer adoption barriers. It looks at:

articles.title: WHEN AGENTS ATTACK: HOW AI COLLAPSES AND REBUILDS MARKETPLACE MOATS
articles.summary: AI agents are collapsing marketplace funnels, disrupting discovery, transactions, and retention. Founders must adapt with agent integration, supply-side control, and a shift to first-purchase economics.

articles.title: MAX MRR: YOUR GROWTH CEILING
articles.summary: Max MRR shows the highest monthly revenue a SaaS business can sustain, based on churn and new customer growth. It's a leading, actionable metric that guides teams to fix churn before investing in more acquisition and highlights when retention, not market size, limits growth.

articles.title: SOURCES OF TRUTH
articles.summary: “Source of truth” issues in product development are rarely about truth itself. They stem from misaligned definitions, competing incentives, and resistance to embracing the complexity inherent in real product work.

articles.title: WHAT MOST LEADERS OF AI GET WRONG ABOUT DRIVING ADOPTION
articles.summary: AI adoption often stalls due to fear, confusion, and lack of involvement, not technical limitations. Leaders must treat it as change management: acknowledge emotions, share transparently, and co-create solutions with teams.

articles.title: HOW TO MAKE COMPETITORS LOOK LIKE ALIENS
articles.summary: Top-performing companies avoid direct comparisons by redefining the game - positioning competitors in narrow roles while claiming broader missions. The key: use simple, truthful analogies that elevate your product into its own category.

articles.title: HOW TO GET RECOMMENDED BY CHATGPT
articles.summary: AI answer engines like ChatGPT and Google are reshaping B2B discovery, replacing search with direct recommendations. To stay visible, brands must optimize for AI prompts with specific content, Reddit mentions, structured comparisons, and influence, not just clicks.

articles.title: EVERYONE IS CRAZY NOW
articles.summary: Social media has turned attention into power, making virality the new path to influence. Today's leaders are often chosen not for competence, but for performance - rewarded for being unhinged, not steady - and the same machine that drives stardom is now shaping the future of politics, tech, and business.

articles.title: WHAT FOUNDERS TEACH US
articles.summary: Founder-led companies thrive on unshakable optimism, risk-taking, and relentless execution. Their fearlessness, curiosity, and conviction attract top talent and drive progress - even through uncertainty - offering lessons any leader can adopt.

articles.title: FROM TOOL TO TEAM: HOW CLAUDE SUBAGENTS LET YOU BUILD STARTUPS IN MARKDOWN
articles.summary: AI is no longer just a tool - it's an organization.

articles.title: HAVING MORE FEATURES IS NOT ALWAYS A MOAT
articles.summary: Moats aren't built by feature count, but by deep, essential usage.
